Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 50 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢šum⸣-ma NUN gišGIGIR U₅-ma KASKAL-MIN ina ⸢DU⸣-šu₂ lu-u₂ giš⸢ŠUDUN⸣

(o 1) If a prince mounts a chariot and on his going on campaign either the yoke or shaft or lance or any little bit of that chariot breaks, and the man, the owner of the chariot, sees (this), discontent will keep on pursuing that prince, the rider of the chariot.

o 22

lu-u₂ ma-šad-du lu-u₂ az-ma-ru-u₂ lu-u₂ mim-ma ḫu-ṣab gišGIGIR ⸢BI⸣

o 33

iš-še-bir-ma LU₂ EN gišGIGIR IGI NUN BI U₅ gišGIGIR la₃ DU₁₀ub lib₃-bi UŠ-MEŠ-šu₂

o 44

ud-da-a-ti IGI-MEŠ šum-ma GISKIM ši-i ana NUN u KUR-šu₂ la₃ TEe

(o 4) He will keep on experiencing afflictions.

(o 4) If that sign is not to approach the prince and his land (and) to avert affliction, so that that prince will achieve his wish wherever he goes and return to his land in good condition from the campaign on which he is going, he will present an offering to the great gods, Nergal and the gods of the open country.

o 55

u₂-da šu-tu-qim-ma NUN BI KI DUku AŠ₂-su i-kaš-ša₂-da

o 66

u ina KASKAL DUku šal-mu-us-su ana KUR-šu₂ GURra ana DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

o 77

dNERGALₓ(U.GUR) u DINGIR EDIN uduSISKUR₂ BALqi₂ DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

(o 7) The great gods, Nergal and the gods of the open country will have mercy on the prince, the rider of the chariot, and they will spare him, [...] they will save him.

o 88

u DINGIR EDIN ana NUN U₅ gišGIGIR ARḪUŠ TUKU-MEŠ-ma iṭ-ṭi-ru-šu₂

o 99

[...] u₂-še!(KUR-)ez!(MA-)zeb₂!(KUM-)bu-šu₂ ⸢NUN⸣ BI IGI dUTU

(o 9) Before Šamaš that prince [...] presents the offering. He performs the apotropaic ritual and [...] saw evil will see calm.

o 1010

[... udu]⸢SISKUR₂⸣ BALqi₂ NAM.BUR₂ DU₃-ma

o 1111

[...] ḪUL IGI pa-ša₂-ḫa IGImar

o 1212

[...] ⸢DINGIR⸣ EDIN KI NUN BI i-sal-li-mu-ma

(o 12) [...] the gods of the open country will be at peace with that prince, and they will give [...] that prince, and they will bind his enemy.

o 1313

[...] ⸢NUN⸣ BI i-nam-di-nu-ma ⸢KUR₂⸣-šu₂ LA₂⸢mu⸣ ana NIG₂.ZU-ka  ; la te-eg-gi

(o 13) Do not be negligent towards your learning!

o 1414

[... u₂]-ba-as-sa-ru KEŠ₂ ana IGI dUTU KEŠ₂

(o 14) [...] he (the prince) brings a message, you set out a ritual assemblage before Šamaš.

o 1515

[...] ⸢im⸣-ta-a-nam EN₂ dUTU EN GALu₂ ša₂ an-na-šu₂

(o 15) You have the prince say the [...] "(Utu,) ... who comes out from the [...]" (and) the incantation "Šamaš, great lord whose consent [...]".

o 1616

[...] ⸢NUN⸣ tu-šad-bab EGIR-šu₂ ana dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

(o 16) After that [...] to Nergal [...].

o 1717

[...] ⸢MAŠ⸣.MAŠ ŠU-su IL₂-ma

(o 17) The mašmaššu-priest raises his hand and [...] "(Nergal,) [...] of the gods, his brothers, pre-eminent one in the lead!"

o 1818

[... DINGIR]-⸢MEŠ⸣ ŠEŠ-MEŠ-šu₂ a-ša₂-re-du ša₂ ⸢maḫ-ri⸣

o 1919

[... ul-la]-⸢šu₂⸣ ul-la

(o 19) "(Šamaš,) [...] his refusal is a refusal.

o 2020

[... ek-le]-⸢ti⸣ dUTU at-ta

(o 20) "You are Šamaš, the [...] of the darkness.

o 2121

[...] ⸢dum⸣-qa

(o 21) "[...] goodness.

o 2222

[...] ⸢GISKIM-MEŠ⸣

(o 22) "[...] signs

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x x

(r 1') [...] ...

r 2'2'

[...] ⸢u₄⸣-mi-⸢šam GUB⸣

(r 2') You set up daily [...].

r 3'3'

[...] x tu-šap-⸢pak?⸣

(r 3') You heap up [...] ....

r 4'4'

[...] ⸢mim⸣-ma lem-nu DINGIR lem-nu d15 ḪULti₃

(r 4') [...].

(r 4') "[...] anything evil, an evil god (and) an evil goddess, tear out (and) move away [...] ruhû-sorcery, rusû-sorcery (and) magical procedures that you know [...]. Let it go off and be kept away from my body.

r 5'5'

[... ru]-⸢ḫe-e⸣ ru-se-e up-ša₂-še-e ša₂ at-ta ZUu₂

r 6'6'

[...] ⸢u₂⸣-suḫ dup-pir lit-tal-lak lip-pa-ris ina ⸢SU-ia₅⸣

r 7'7'

[... ARAD]-⸢ka⸣ da₃-li₂-li₂-ka lud-⸢lul⸣

(r 7') "[...] your servant, let me sing your praises."

r 8'8'

[...] ⸢U₅⸣-ma TA gišGIGIR ŠUBut ana NAM.BUR₂.BI BUR₂-⸢ma⸣

(r 8') [...] mounts [...] and falls from the chariot, for the apotropaic ritual to release (the omen) and his evil not to reach the king or prince and to save him, the mašmaššu-priest (and) the king or prince cleanse themselves for 3 days.

r 9'9'

LUGAL u ⸢NUN ḪUL⸣-šu₂ la ka-ša₂-di u KAR-šu₂ U₄ 3-KAM MAŠ.MAŠ LUGAL u NUN uq-⸢ta⸣-ad-da-šu₂

r 10'10'

ina GU₂ ID₂ KI SAR A KU₃ SU₃ ana!(EN₂) dAMAR.UTU dUTU DINGIR IRI u d15 IRI

(r 10') On the bank of a watercourse you sweep the ground. You sprinkle pure water. You set up assorted censers to Marduk, Šamaš, the city's god and the city's goddess, (and) his god and his goddess. You present a pure offering.

r 11'11'

DINGIR-šu₂ u d15-šu₂ NIG₂.NA-ME šam-ḫu-tu₂ GARan uduSISKUR₂ eb₂-ba BALqi₂

r 12'12'

⸢1 GIN₂⸣-TA.AM₃ dugME.ḪE₂ ana UDUB₃ GARan ŠIM-ḪI.A ZI₃.MAD.GA₂ DUBaq

(r 12') You put 1 shekel each of fatty tissue on the charcoal. You strew aromatics (and) flour.

r 13'13'

[...] NIG₂.BA ana DINGIR-ME ša₂-šu-nu GARan-ma UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

(r 13') [...]. You set in place gifts to those gods and he speaks as follows.

r 14'14'

[... al]-⸢si⸣-ku-nu-šu₂ ana ṣi-it pi-ia u₂-taq-qa₂-a

(r 14') "[...], I have called out to you. Attend to the utterance of my mouth.

r 15'15'

[...] ⸢an?⸣-ni? aḫ-ṭu-u₂ e-še-ʾu-u u₂-gal-li-lu

(r 15') "[...]. [...] the sins that I have committed, that I have perpetrated, that I have been responsible for, let me be on my guard."

r 16'16'

[...] ⸢lut-ta⸣-aṣ-ra 7-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA KAŠ SAG ša₂ tu-kin-nu BALqi₂

(r 16') He says (this) 7 times. You libate first-class beer that you have set up.

r 17'17'

[x x u₂IN.NU].⸢UŠ⸣ ina I₃.GIŠ BARA₂.GA ŠEŠ₂-su-ma GISKIM-MEŠ  ; ḪUL-MEŠ NU TE-MEŠ-šu₂

(r 17') You anoint him with [...] maštakal-plant in filtered oil and the bad signs will not approach him.

r 18'18'

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ NA GAL₂ši ina E₂ KI IZI IGIru

(r 18') "[...] exists [...] a man's house, in the house where the fire appeared."

r 19'19'

[x mBAša₂-a bu-kur₂ m]dINANA—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir

(r 19') [...] of Iqiša, the son of Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir.

r 20'20'

[...] x x x x ⸢pa?-liḫ?⸣ d60 u an-⸢tu₄ la₃ IR?⸣-šu₂

(r 20') [...] .... One who is reverent toward Anu and Antu should not take it (the tablet) away.
